steps.title: Factor as a difference of squares
steps.content:
The equation $x^2 - 49 = 0$ contains a difference of squares: $x^2 - 7^2$. This factors as:
$$x^2 - 49 = (x - 7)(x + 7) = 0$$

steps.title: Solve and count the solutions
steps.content:
From $(x - 7)(x + 7) = 0$, we have two linear factors. Setting each equal to zero:
  • $x - 7 = 0$ gives $x = 7$
  • $x + 7 = 0$ gives $x = -7$
These are two distinct real solutions. That matches choice C — C is correct.
